rows.risk: Pressure-free clogging index could be misread as a pressure-calibrated safety criterion.
rows.reviewer_concern: Chemical Engineering Science reviewers may ask whether Ib is supported by pressure-drop or CFD data.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript states that Ib is a pressure-free structural pre-screening index and that the Ergun result is only a proxy.
rows.mitigation_action: Keep the current boundary language; upgrade only after CFD/OpenLB/OpenFOAM or measured pressure-gradient data are added.

rows.risk: Critical clogging transition is not demonstrated by the current data.
rows.reviewer_concern: A reviewer could reject any claim of a universal transition because connectivity loss is not measurable in the current cases.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript frames the result as pre-clogging migration/filtering and states that identifying a bed-scale clogging threshold requires higher loading, localized release, or coupled flow.
rows.mitigation_action: Do not claim critical transition in title, abstract, or conclusions unless new localized/high-loading production runs generate connectivity loss or pressure increase.

rows.risk: Loading scan has three single-seed states and cannot support a monotonic law.
rows.reviewer_concern: The final BTC values are not monotonic with injected debris count, so reviewers may challenge trend claims.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript says the loading data are bounded loading responses rather than a monotonic breakthrough law.
rows.mitigation_action: For a stronger paper, add repeat seeds or reframe the loading scan as a bounded response only.

rows.risk: Localized internal-release cases may be overread as a source-position or source-strength law.
rows.reviewer_concern: The localized-release evidence now covers the planned target-time rows, but the case family is still too sparse to fit a general source-position or source-strength law.
rows.current_boundary: 3/3 localized-release production jobs reached target physical time; the manuscript treats them as mechanism evidence rather than a fitted source-location law.
rows.mitigation_action: Keep localized-release interpretation bounded to retention, downstream leakage and sparse-front decoupling; add more source positions/seeds only if a scaling law is claimed.

rows.risk: DEM-derived pore fields could be mistaken for experimental CT validation.
rows.reviewer_concern: The study has no physical CT/PIV experiment, so imaging terminology must be precise.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript uses DEM-derived pore reconstruction and explicitly rejects experimental computed-tomography interpretation.
rows.mitigation_action: Keep CT out of the title and figure labels; use 'DEM-derived pore reconstruction' or 'voxelized pore field'.

rows.risk: One-way Stokes drag omits pore-scale gas redistribution and two-way coupling.
rows.reviewer_concern: Reviewers may ask whether drag forcing is physically realistic inside a tortuous packed bed.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript states that the forcing is one-way Stokes drag, reports a dimensional drag-scaling audit, and does not solve the local gas field.
rows.mitigation_action: Do not amplify drag nonphysically; use the S18 Reynolds/drag-to-weight audit for scope, and add CFD/OpenLB/OpenFOAM or calibrated pore-flow closure only for resolved gas-flow claims.

rows.risk: Large-pebble motion during debris transport would confound deposition interpretation.
rows.reviewer_concern: If the skeleton moves, transport differences could reflect bed rearrangement rather than debris migration.
rows.current_boundary: Formal transport cases use a fixed compacted skeleton so the response is attributed to debris parameters.
rows.mitigation_action: Keep this statement in Methods and verify future LIGGGHTS cases freeze type-1 pebbles.

rows.risk: Figure panels could overstate evidence or look like schematic claims rather than data.
rows.reviewer_concern: Figures with text-heavy panels, black slices, or unclear provenance weaken a mechanism-first journal submission.
rows.current_boundary: A figure provenance manifest records source scripts, exports, and claim boundaries for each figure.
rows.mitigation_action: Use the provenance manifest before final figure edits and avoid large in-figure text blocks.

rows.risk: Title and framing could sound like an experiment or a critical-clogging paper.
rows.reviewer_concern: The target journal title should be concise and should not promise unavailable validation or critical transition evidence.
rows.current_boundary: The current title is Chemical Engineering Science-oriented and DEM-bounded: Separating breakthrough from pore-network clogging indicators in gas-driven fine-debris transport through Li4SiO4 pebble beds.
rows.mitigation_action: Do not reintroduce experiment, CT, or critical transition wording unless the evidence changes.

rows.risk: Reduced hydraulic sensitivity could be mistaken for resolved CFD pressure evidence.
rows.reviewer_concern: Reviewers may ask whether the 3D resistance-amplification result is a calibrated pore-flow or pressure-drop calculation.
rows.current_boundary: The manuscript and figure provenance identify Fig. S22/Table S15 as a scalar voxel-network sensitivity test, not OpenFOAM CFD, LBM, measured pressure or pressure-calibrated Ib.
rows.mitigation_action: Keep Fig. S22 in the supplement and do not promote it to validation-grade pressure evidence until a coupled flow solver consumes the resistance field.

rows.reviewer_concern: Journal submission requires data availability, funding, competing-interest, and acknowledgement statements.
rows.mitigation_action: Confirm repository DOI/accession, funding, competing interests, acknowledgements, author email/ORCID, and AI disclosure before submission.
